2.  The "flooding" is manifest as clear, transparent liquid widely spread across the white workbench surface, creating distinct shiny puddles and wet streaks. This liquid also covers parts of the black, rectangular component (likely a cell plate or housing) being manipulated, as well as the beige manifold it connects to, making these surfaces appear reflective and saturated with fluid.

These clear accumulations are particularly noticeable in the mid-left and lower-right areas of the sheet. Additionally, there is a dark, likely viscous, liquid visible on the edges and bottom of the black rectangular component being held, and as a dark residue around the beige fluidic connectors attached to the main silver component.
